source_paper_title: Upper Bounds for All and Max-Gain Policy Iteration Algorithms on Deterministic MDPs
context:
Page 31, Section 6 (Summary and outlook), first bullet under “Our results give rise to several questions for further investigation.”

“It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with k ≥ 3 actions. … A concrete first step in pursuit of a ‘Hamiltonian’ lower bound could be to attempt constructing a 3-state, 3-action MDP in which all 27 policies can be visited by PI.”
title: Hamiltonian policy-iteration path for multi-action MDPs (k ≥ 3)
latex:
Given a discounted finite Markov decision process (MDP) \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) with \(|S|=n\ge 2\), \(|A|=k\ge 3\), transition probabilities \(T(s,a,s')\in[0,1]\) satisfying \(\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')=1\), rewards \(R(s,a)\in\mathbb{R}\), and discount factor \(\gamma\in[0,1)\), let \(\Pi=A^S\) be the set of deterministic stationary policies.

For a policy \(\pi\in\Pi\), let \(V^\pi:S\to\mathbb{R}\) be its discounted value function, i.e. the unique solution to the Bellman equations
\[V^\pi(s)=R(s,\pi(s))+\gamma\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,\pi(s),s')V^\pi(s')\quad \forall s\in S.\]
Define the gain of switching state \(s\) from \(\pi(s)\) to action \(a\in A\) by
\[\rho_\pi(s,a)=Q^\pi(s,a)-V^\pi(s),\]
where \(Q^\pi(s,a)=R(s,a)+\gamma\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')V^\pi(s')\). A one-step policy-improvement move from \(\pi\) to \(\pi'\) is allowed if there exists a nonempty set \(S_{\mathrm{switch}}\subseteq S\) and actions \(a_s\in A\) for \(s\in S_{\mathrm{switch}}\) such that \(\rho_\pi(s,a_s)>0\) for all \(s\in S_{\mathrm{switch}}\), and
\[\pi'(s)=\begin{cases}a_s,& s\in S_{\mathrm{switch}},\\ \pi(s),& s\notin S_{\mathrm{switch}}.\end{cases}\]
This defines the policy-improvement directed acyclic graph (PI-DAG) whose vertices are \(\Pi\) and whose edges are all such improvement moves.

Problem: Determine whether for every \(k\ge 3\) there exists an MDP \(M\) (for some \(n\ge 2\), possibly depending on \(k\)) whose PI-DAG contains a directed Hamiltonian path, i.e. a directed path that visits every policy in \(\Pi\) exactly once. Equivalently, does there exist an MDP with \(|A|=k\ge 3\) and a policy-iteration switching rule such that policy iteration visits all \(k^n\) deterministic stationary policies before terminating at an optimal policy?
location_in_paper: Page 31, Section 6 (Summary and outlook), first bullet under “Our results give rise to several questions for further investigation.”
open_problem_quote: “It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with k ≥ 3 actions. … A concrete first step in pursuit of a ‘Hamiltonian’ lower bound could be to attempt constructing a 3-state, 3-action MDP in which all 27 policies can be visited by PI.”
subject_classification: Stochastic Dynamic Programming / Computational Complexity of MDP Algorithms
